name: BAHRMAN LAW LLC
address: 985 SW DISK DR STE 120 & 130, BEND, OR 97702

fr.recommendations: Enhancing the visibility in and around the property by managing dense vegetation could improve emergency responder access and operational effectiveness. By strategically thinning or trimming the vegetation, responders may have an easier time navigating the property, which can facilitate better movement and response times. Additionally, ensuring that pedestrian walkways and paths through the landscaped areas are well-marked and unobstructed may help responders quickly reach key areas of the property. Improving sightlines from the main building and vehicle parking area to open green spaces could support more efficient staging and coordination efforts in emergency scenarios. These adjustments can assist in creating a safer and more navigable environment for responders working under urgent conditions.

wildfire.recommendations: Improving wildfire resilience could involve increasing the separation between vegetation and structures, particularly by managing the dense shrub cover adjacent to the building and along the fence. Reducing the vegetation density in these areas can enhance defensible space and may limit the continuity of fuels around the property. Managing the vegetated perimeter by selectively thinning scattered trees and shrubs may create additional buffer zones that diminish potential fire intensity near the residence. Maintaining open spaces around the structure and ensuring that any combustible brush near critical infrastructure is carefully managed could further support this. Establishing clearer boundaries between vegetation and access routes would also improve conditions for firefighter access and suppression efforts during a wildfire event. Consistent and strategic vegetation management will contribute to long-term property preparedness and support efforts to minimize wildfire exposure.
